name: Maple Shortcake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, salt, and mace.
2. Using your fingertips, work in the softened butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Gradually add milk to form a soft dough. Knead the dough 5-6 times.
4. Divide the dough into two equal portions. Grease two 8-inch pie plates.
5. Press each portion of dough into the prepared pie plates, forming a cake-like layer.
6. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15-18 minutes, or until lightly browned.
7. Remove from the oven and let cool.
Filling:
1. In a saucepan, combine maple syrup and butter over medium heat.
2. Cook until it forms a soft ball when dropped into ice water (238°F / 114°C on a candy thermometer).
3. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
4. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until stiff peaks form.
5. Gradually pour the hot syrup mixture into the beaten egg whites, beating constantly with an electric mixer until smooth and thick.
6. If using a hand rotary beater, add small amounts of syrup, beat, then add more until all is incorporated.
7. Fold in the whipped cream and vanilla extract.
Assembly:
1. Spread filling between the biscuit layers and on top of one layer.
